input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule IC.2.5.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ic.2.5.3
            TITLE: plenums must not be located anywhere upstream of the throttle body

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.2.5.3: Plenums must not be located anywhere upstream of the throttle body
IC.2.5.3 Plenums must not be located anywhere upstream of the throttle body 
For the purpose of definition, a plenum is any tank or volume that is a significant enlargement 
of the normal intake runner system.  Teams may submit their designs via a Rules Question for 
review prior to competition if the legality of their proposed system is in doubt.

final_answer: rule IC.2.5.3: Plenums must not be located anywhere upstream of the throttle body For the purpose of definition, a plenum is any tank or volume that is a significant enlargement of the normal intake runner system.
